name: NetSuite Finance Demo: Standard Reporting and Drilldown

avoid: custom saved reports for prospect demos
deep links unless used as fallback after a learned route
navigationPolicy.reportPolicy: Use standard NetSuite reports for prospect-facing demos unless the user explicitly asks for a custom report.

login.instructions: Open the browser window, sign in to NetSuite, and leave it on any authenticated page. The runner waits for the global Search box before starting.

demoRequest.topic: Generate a finance demo from the pre-demo notes. Focus on standard income statement and profitability reporting, drilldown from summary to transaction detail, export options and fewer spreadsheet handoffs and subsidiary and consolidated finance views. Demo scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..
demoRequest.demoScope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management.
demoRequest.manifestDemoModeInstruction: Turn the manifest into a customer-centered story. Use the selected audience and target audience playbook to personalize narration, proof moments, and the SC runbook.
demoRequest.demoStrategyInstruction: Demo strategy: Standard Platform Demo. Keep the tone polished, structured, and business-focused, pacing balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first, technical depth light to moderate unless requested, and story style broad end-to-end platform story connecting multiple stakeholder perspectives. Prioritize end-to-end business flows, cross-functional workflows, executive dashboards, reporting and analytics and automation examples. Behavior rules: prioritize breadth over deep specialization, ensure all major departments are represented, maintain consistent pacing, avoid excessive technical depth unless requested and make executive-level value visible throughout. Adapt the examples to General Business using finance visibility, operational control and growth readiness.
demoRequest.industryInstruction: Industry playbook: General Business. Use terminology such as finance visibility, operational control and growth readiness, emphasize KPIs such as cash position, profitability, close speed and forecast accuracy, and avoid industry-specific claims without discovery and unsupported benchmark statements.
demoRequest.outputLanguageInstruction: Write SC-facing output, setup guidance, and narrator talk tracks in English.
demoRequest.audienceInstruction: Demo to the Prospect audience type in a Mid-Market target audience context. Potential buyers evaluating whether the solution solves their business challenges and delivers measurable value. Focus on business value, ROI, ease of adoption and competitive differentiation. Include business pain points, before/after scenarios, success stories and metrics and outcomes. Avoid deep technical configuration, feature dumping, internal terminology and long setup explanations. For Mid-Market, use a process-oriented, business-value focused and real-world operational scenarios demo style. Prioritize process efficiency, visibility, integration and cross-team collaboration; include role-based workflows, analytics dashboards, integrations and approval processes; avoid oversimplified startup messaging, feature dumping, extremely technical implementation details and unnecessary enterprise governance complexity.
demoRequest.instructions:
Lead with the customer's business problem and desired outcome before showing screens.
Open with the biggest business hitters first: executive visibility, trusted numbers, consolidation confidence, close speed, project profitability, auditability, approvals, and fewer spreadsheets.
Use a crisp tell-show-tell rhythm: frame the point, show the shortest proof path, then land why it matters in plain language.
Use NetSuite global search and the navigation bar as the primary way to move through the product.
Use standard NetSuite reports for prospect-facing finance demos unless the audience explicitly asks for custom reporting.
Choose the optimal demo flow and put the highest-impact moments first.
Start with a general executive overview of NetSuite before moving into the specific workflow.
Keep SuiteProjects and Fixed Asset Management within the agreed scope, but do not let them overshadow the primary financials story.
Avoid feature tours, menu wandering, slow setup, dead clicks, and deep configuration unless intentionally requested.
Never announce internal labels such as value statement during narration.

additionalDemoSources.label: Use Standard NetSuite Reports First
additionalDemoSources.content: For prospect-facing finance demos, prioritize standard NetSuite reports and dashboards before custom reporting or saved-search detail.
additionalDemoSources.guidance: Use this as a routing and narration rule when the demo touches financial reporting, P&L, drilldown, export, or finance visibility.
additionalDemoSources.logic: If the demo asks for reporting or finance visibility, keep standard reports in the primary path and park custom reporting as an optional follow-up.

demoRequest.instruction: Use NetSuite navigation/search first, use standard reports for prospect-facing demos, and keep custom report links only as explicit fallbacks. Start with a short general or executive NetSuite overview, then order the demo from highest-value proof moments to supporting detail. Demo scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management. Demo to the Prospect audience type in a Mid-Market target audience context. Potential buyers evaluating whether the solution solves their business challenges and delivers measurable value. Focus on business value, ROI, ease of adoption and competitive differentiation. Include business pain points, before/after scenarios, success stories and metrics and outcomes. Avoid deep technical configuration, feature dumping, internal terminology and long setup explanations. For Mid-Market, use a process-oriented, business-value focused and real-world operational scenarios demo style. Prioritize process efficiency, visibility, integration and cross-team collaboration; include role-based workflows, analytics dashboards, integrations and approval processes; avoid oversimplified startup messaging, feature dumping, extremely technical implementation details and unnecessary enterprise governance complexity. Demo strategy: Standard Platform Demo. Keep the tone polished, structured, and business-focused, pacing balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first, technical depth light to moderate unless requested, and story style broad end-to-end platform story connecting multiple stakeholder perspectives. Prioritize end-to-end business flows, cross-functional workflows, executive dashboards, reporting and analytics and automation examples. Behavior rules: prioritize breadth over deep specialization, ensure all major departments are represented, maintain consistent pacing, avoid excessive technical depth unless requested and make executive-level value visible throughout. Adapt the examples to General Business using finance visibility, operational control and growth readiness. Additional Admin logic to consider: Use Standard NetSuite Reports First: Use this as a routing and narration rule when the demo touches financial reporting, P&L, drilldown, export, or finance visibility.

company.title: Private Jet & Cargo Charters - Air Charter Service
company.description: Global leader in private jet, helicopter, airliner & cargo aircraft charters. Over 25,000 charters per year, offices worldwide, contact us 24/7.

codexPrepAnalysis.analysis:
# Codex Prep Operator Analysis
Task title: created by demo helper - Private Jet & Cargo Charters

## Company Context That Should Shape The Demo
- Air Charter Service is a global aircraft charter brokerage, not an aircraft operator; finance should see charter/booking economics clearly without implying owned aircraft operations.
- The public site currently states **35,000+ flights per year**, **40+ offices across 6 continents**, and access to **50,000 aircraft**, so the demo should emphasize scale, entity growth, currencies, local reporting, and control.
- The business spans private jet, group charter, cargo, emergency/time-critical, humanitarian, dangerous goods, and specialist sectors, which implies varied booking types, margins, vendors, and operational metadata.
- The operating model depends on 24/7 dedicated account managers, local office knowledge, and post-flight detail capture, so finance needs transaction context from bookings, not just GL totals.
- ACS presents financial transparency and annual revenue above $1B on its financials page, making consolidated reporting confidence and auditability especially important.
- Cargo services reference permits, clearances, trucking, customs partners, aircraft documents, and insurance checks, suggesting approvals and documentation context are likely business-relevant.

## Likely ERP Priorities
- Consolidated executive visibility across countries, entities, currencies, and business lines.
- Faster, trusted close with drilldown from group financials to local entity and transaction detail.
- Reduced spreadsheet dependency for consolidation, FP&A handoffs, fixed assets, and management reporting.
- Flight or booking-level profitability: revenue, vendor costs, customer, broker, route, aircraft type, timing, and margin.
- Approval workflows with enough context for approvers to understand what they are approving.
- Working-capital visibility, especially AR, dunning, payment delays, and incomplete billing data.
- Scalable finance architecture that can support local reporting, tax, e-invoicing, and new-country expansion.
- Controlled integration path from CRM or booking references into finance without making the first demo too technical.

## Recommended Demo Direction
Start with a short executive overview: NetSuite as one finance platform for global visibility, trusted numbers, approvals, reporting, and drilldown. Lead with the business problem: ACS-like scale creates pressure when bookings, entities, close activities, approvals, assets, and management reporting are split across systems and spreadsheets.

The highest-value proof path should be:
1. Executive dashboard or standard financial reporting view showing consolidated performance.
2. Standard Income Statement / P&L with subsidiary, department, class, location, or project-style views.
3. Drill from summary to transaction detail to prove confidence in the numbers.
4. Cash 360 / AR visibility to connect incomplete data and payment delays to action.
5. Approval queue or transaction approval example with booking context visible.
6. SuiteProjects as a controlled profitability lens for charter bookings, not a full project-management deep dive.
7. Fixed Asset Management last, framed as moving office equipment and intangible asset tracking out of spreadsheets.

Use NetSuite global search and navigation bar for movement. Keep the story financials-first and use SuiteProjects/FAM as supporting proof points.

## Scope And Account Prep Implications
- Demo account needs multiple subsidiaries, currencies, intercompany/consolidation-ready structure, and credible local entity examples.
- Standard reports should be prepared: Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow or cash visibility, AR aging, and transaction drilldowns.
- Add mock charter booking/project records with route, start/end date, customer, vendor, aircraft type, broker, revenue, and costs.
- Prepare transactions linked to bookings: customer invoice, vendor bill, expenses, payment status, and approval state.
- Configure sample approval thresholds and approver roles with enough memo/custom-field context to make approvals meaningful.
- Include a small fixed asset register with straight-line depreciation examples for office equipment and intangible assets.
- Prepare saved searches or standard list views only where needed to show booking context; avoid making custom reporting the main story.
- Have a simple integration talk track ready: CRM/booking reference flows into finance as controlled master/transaction data, with technical detail deferred.

## Discovery Gaps That Could Hurt The Demo
- Active countries, currencies, local statutory reporting needs, and required local languages.
- Which countries require e-invoicing send/receive and what tax localization expectations exist.
- Current ERP, consolidation, planning, expenses, CRM, and booking-reference architecture.
- Whether consolidation and FP&A replacement are phase-one goals or longer-term outcomes.
- Executive success metrics: close days, reporting confidence, spreadsheet reduction, audit readiness, working-capital improvement.
- Whether SuiteProjects should be positioned as phase one or as a future controlled extension.
- Whether Fixed Asset Management is initial scope or only a prepared extension.
- Demo timebox and stakeholder attendance, especially CFO, Controller, IT, Transformation, and Operations.

Sources used: Air Charter Service [homepage](https://www.aircharterservice.com/), [How ACS Works](https://www.aircharterservice.com/about-us/how-air-charter-service-works), [Cargo Charter](https://www.aircharterservice.com/cargo-charter/), and [Financials](https://www.aircharterservice.com/about-us/financials).
codexPrepAnalysis.sessionTitle: created by demo helper - Private Jet & Cargo Charters

context.preDemoNotes:
MOCK DEAL DATA ONLY - for NetSuite Demo Helper product demonstration.
Customer website: https://www.aircharterservice.com/
Audience: Prospect, mid-market, standard platform demo, general business.
Demo scope: Financials first Services premium. Include core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight/booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management for office equipment and intangible assets.

Mock stakeholder map:
- Group CFO wants consolidated visibility, stronger confidence in group reporting, and fewer spreadsheet handoffs.
- Financial Controller wants trusted close reporting, local entity views, local GAAP considerations, and better drilldown from summary to detail.
- Finance Transformation Manager wants a scalable ERP foundation that can grow beyond a UK-centric process.
- IT Director wants integration fit, architecture clarity, and controlled data flow from CRM/booking references into finance.
- Operations stakeholder wants flight or booking-level context visible enough for finance to understand revenue, cost, route, aircraft type, and broker involvement.

Mock current-state notes:
- The prospect initially explored procure-to-pay, then widened the conversation into a broader ERP evaluation.
- Current process is fragmented across a UK-focused ERP setup, external consolidation/planning, spreadsheets, email-heavy invoice handling, and disconnected expense processes.
- Multi-country growth creates issues with new companies, local reporting, tax, e-invoicing, and chart-of-account/local GAAP requirements.
- Consolidation and FP&A are handled outside the ERP today; the demo should show how NetSuite can reduce dependency on external spreadsheets and disconnected reporting.
- Each flight or charter booking behaves like a project: route, start/end date, aircraft type, broker, customer, vendor costs, revenue, and associated transactions need to be explainable.
- The team wants to understand how SuiteProjects could support services/project-style profitability without turning the first demo into a deep implementation workshop.
- Fixed assets are currently tracked in spreadsheets with straight-line depreciation; show Fixed Asset Management as part of the prepared scope, but keep it behind the biggest finance proof moments.
- Approvals and thresholds exist, but approvers may not always understand what they are approving.
- Dunning and payment delays are often caused by incomplete data and lack of centralized context.
- Integration with existing CRM or booking references matters, but the first demo should keep integration discussion business-facing rather than technical.

Mock open discovery gaps to surface in Intelligence:
- Confirm active countries, currencies, local language needs, and which countries require e-invoicing send/receive.
- Confirm the current ERP, consolidation, planning, expense, and CRM architecture.
- Confirm executive success metrics for the ERP project.
- Confirm whether SuiteProjects should be positioned as phase one or controlled future scope.
- Confirm whether Fixed Asset Management is in initial scope or shown as a prepared extension.
- Confirm demo timebox and stakeholder attendance.

demoStrategy.description: A broad, structured product demonstration that showcases the overall platform, major capabilities, and end-to-end business value within a fixed timeframe.
demoStrategy.pacing: balanced and time-aware, with high-value workflows first

avoid excessive technical depth unless requested
connect workflows through one narrative story where possible

items.label: sample purchase order

items.reason: needed if the story requires controls, approvals, or role-based access

items.reason: needed if fixed assets are in scope for the personalized story or setup prompt

items.label: planning and budgeting demo context
items.reason: needed if FP&A, budgets, or planning are in scope for the demo

items.label: services SKU or project demo context
items.reason: needed if the scoped demo should show services-led financials or project profitability

manifestBuild.instruction: The SC guide has been generated. Create the runnable manifest from the guide before relying on the Manifest tab or browser rehearsal.

topicPrompt.label: Demo topic
topicPrompt.placeholder: Example: finance demo for P&L, drilldown, export, and Cash 360
topicPrompt.output: Create or amend this manifest before rehearsal.

slider.label: Light
slider.description: Add value points at the big transitions only.

slider.label: Balanced
slider.description: Add value points on each major page or section.

slider.label: Heavy
slider.description: Add value points throughout the demo.

runButtons.label: Dry Run
runButtons.description: Print the planned demo without opening NetSuite.

runButtons.label: Rehearse And Prep Account
runButtons.description: Create the account prep buffer, then run the browser without audio, verify pages, save timings and screenshots.

runButtons.label: Run Live Demo
runButtons.description: Run the browser automation with narration.

segments.title: Frame The Executive NetSuite Overview
segments.objective: Set up the general NetSuite platform story before opening detailed finance workflows.
segments.valueStatement: Private Jet & Cargo Charters first sees why NetSuite matters at the executive level: visibility, control, trusted numbers, and a clear path from summary to action.
segments.narration: Before we open the first report, frame the executive view for Private Jet & Cargo Charters: NetSuite is the operating system where finance can see performance, prove the detail, and make decisions from one controlled place. We will start with the highest-value proof moments, then move into supporting detail. Keep the live path inside this scope: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..

actions.text: Executive overview. Audience: Prospect. Target: Mid-Market. Strategy: Standard Platform Demo. Industry: General Business. Stay inside this demo scope unless the SC deliberately branches: Financials first Services premium, including core financials, multi-entity reporting, approvals, SuiteProjects for flight or booking profitability, and Fixed Asset Management..

segments.title: Open The Standard Income Statement
segments.objective: Use NetSuite search/navigation to open a standard profitability report.
segments.valueStatement: Private Jet & Cargo Charters gets a fast, trusted performance view that connects directly to trusted reporting and drilldown from summary to detail.
segments.narration: For Prospect, keep the narration persuasive, outcome-oriented and story-driven and anchored in business value, ROI, ease of adoption and competitive differentiation. For Private Jet & Cargo Charters, let's start where a finance leader usually wants to start: the standard income statement. This gives a quick view of revenue, margin, overheads, and net profit from one controlled place in NetSuite.

segments.title: Read The Finance Story
segments.objective: Show the CFO-level view: sales, gross profit, operating profit, and net result.
segments.valueStatement: A first-time viewer can understand performance without needing to know where every transaction lives.
segments.narration: For someone new to the system, this report is intentionally familiar. Turnover is at the top, overheads sit below it, and operating profit and net profit are easy to find at the bottom.

segments.title: Show Report Filters
segments.objective: Highlight the filter set finance can use to re-slice the result.
segments.valueStatement: Private Jet & Cargo Charters can re-run the same standard report by period, subsidiary, and accounting book, which supports trusted reporting and drilldown from summary to detail.
segments.narration: Now let's look at how a team can change the lens. In the standard report, the most important controls are period, date range, subsidiary context, and accounting book.

segments.title: Drill Into The Numbers
segments.objective: Move from summary P&L to the transaction-level support behind a number.
segments.valueStatement: When a number is questioned, the team can investigate it from the report instead of starting a separate reconciliation exercise.
segments.narration: If someone asks what sits behind a number, we can drill from the summary into the supporting detail. That turns the report from a static total into an explainable result.
fallback: If the drilldown route changes, click a blue linked report amount or account line such as Revenue, then return to the saved report.

segments.title: Show Export Options
segments.objective: Show how the report can be shared or taken offline.
segments.valueStatement: Teams can keep NetSuite as the source of truth while still producing the files people expect for reviews, audits, and board packs.
segments.narration: When the report needs to leave the system, the export options are already here. Excel, PDF, CSV, Word, and print are available without rebuilding the report somewhere else.
